patches.find: The dome adopts a 400+ year observational fact — westward magnetic drift has been documented since Edmund Halley's late-17th/early-18th-century declination surveys
patches.replace: The dome adopts a ~390-year observational fact — westward magnetic drift has been documented since Edmund Halley's late-17th/early-18th-century declination surveys
patches.rationale: c4 minor finding: 400+ year is arithmetic overreach. 2026-1635 (Gellibrand) = 391 years; 2026-1701 (Halley Atlantic Chart) = 325 years. '~390-year' is accurate to Gellibrand as the original discoverer, and matches the c3 review's own prose ('~390 years'). The c3 patch updated 'centuries-old' to 'late-17th/early-18th-century Halley' — but the '400+' lead-in was not corrected at that time and is now inconsistent.

patches.find: It has no core-dynamics mechanism to predict drift rate or direction independently.
patches.replace: It has no core-flow mechanism to predict drift rate or direction independently. Both the dome model and standard MHD are consistent with westward drift; only standard MHD derives the rate quantitatively from first principles (Finlay 2016: ~0.2°/yr from CMB flow inversions).
patches.rationale: Combines c4 minor finding ('core-dynamics' → 'core-flow', matching detail_evidence wording) with ISS-1865 singleton fix (add one sentence distinguishing consistency from derivation, as recommended in both c3 and c4 reviews). The ISS-1865 sentence closes defense_survives=3 — a dome defender cannot argue 'you treat us no worse than standard MHD' once we explicitly note standard MHD derives the rate quantitatively while the dome predicts no specific rate. Single atomic patch covers both the stylistic micro-fix and the defense-closing addition.
